Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
Off Calendar Jun-07-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Philip T. Kiduff, Tel. No. 1-415-673-0368 specially appearing for the Defendants;
No other appearances on the matter.
RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Believe Wireless, LLC's and Paul Dowling's motion to quash service of summons for lack of personal jurisdiction is granted. Plaintiff Ivy Growth Holdings, LLC has not shown that defendants directed their activities at California residents or purposefully availed themselves of the benefits of the California. (Vons Companies, Inc. v. Seabest Foods, Inc. (1996) 14 Cal.4th 434, 446). Believe Wireless does not advertise in California, does not provide services in California, does not maintain an office in California and is not registered to do business in California. The fact that defendants entered into a contract with a California resident company does not establish that defendants have sufficient minimum contacts with California. The court is required to examine "prior negotiations and contemplated future consequences, along with the terms of the contract and the parties' actual course of dealing." (Burger King Corp. v. Rudzewicz (1985) 471 U.S. 462, 478 - 479). Ivy reached out to defendants in Maryland with an offer for a business transaction and the evidence on this motion shows that negotiations occurred via email between the parties. The contract does not envisage that Believe Wireless or Ivy perform any services in California. The contract provides that, in exchange for acquiring plaintiff's Maryland client, Believe Wireless would pay Ivy a monthly sum as long as it continues to provide services to that client in Maryland. Defendants' only conduct connected to California, besides contracting with a California entity, is the monthly payment required by the contract. The circumstances surrounding the contract do not show that defendants had the requisite minimum contacts with California to justify this court's exercise of personal jurisdiction over them. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC
3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR # 8600 =302/hk
